special_rules.name: TS1 Strategy Rating
special_rules.text: A Thousand Sons Chaos Space Marine army has a Strategy rating of 5.

special_rules.name: TS2 Initiative Rating
special_rules.text: Aircraft and Silver Tower formations have an initiative rating of 2+. All other formations have 1+.

special_rules.name: TS3 Summoned Units
special_rules.text: Unbroken formations with Daemonic Pact may summon daemons from the Daemon Pool by rolling 2D6 summoning points. The allegiance of the summoning formation determines what may be summoned. Summoned units are placed within 5cm of the formation, may not be chained, may not be placed in enemy zone of control or impassable terrain, and count as part of the formation while they remain in play. If the formation is broken, summoned units are removed from play. In the End phase, after rally tests, summoned daemons return to the Daemon Pool; war engine daemons keep their current DC when they return.

special_rules.name: TS4 Augment Summoning
special_rules.text: Units with Augmented Summoning (+x) add x summoning points to the roll when the formation summons daemons.

special_rules.name: TS5 Rubric
special_rules.text: Units affected by the Rubric of Ahriman are treated as automatons. They may not take March actions, and when making a withdrawal move they may only make a single move rather than two. A Rubric unit’s special rule is one-shot only when it is listed as such.

special_rules.name: TS6 Daemonic Focus
special_rules.text: Units with Daemonic Focus may keep summoned units in play after the formation rallies, but not if the formation is broken.

special_rules.name: TS7 Fickle Masters
special_rules.text: In the strategy phase, if the Chaos player rolls a 6, add 1D3 lesser daemons to the Daemon Pool. If the Chaos player rolls a 1, remove D3 lesser daemons from the Daemon Pool. If no daemons remain in the Daemon Pool, there is no further effect.

special_rules.name: TS8 Webway Portal
special_rules.text: Wraithgate is a webway portal reference item. It may not be destroyed. Only formations consisting completely of infantry units, light vehicle units, or armoured vehicle units with the Walker ability may use a Wraithgate to enter play.

profiles.name: Wraithgate
abilities_or_notes: Only formations consisting completely of infantry units, light vehicle units, or armoured vehicle units with the Walker ability may use a Wraithgate to enter play.
parse_warnings: Reference-only entry reconstructed from the live list and portal rules.
ambiguity_reasons: No Thousand Sons-specific Wraithgate profile was available in the local source files.

profiles.name: Thousand Sons Sorcerer
parse_warnings: Older compendium source labels this role as Preceptor/Sorceror in some drafts.
ambiguity_reasons: The current live list uses 'Sorcerer' while older sources use 'Preceptor' wording.

profiles.name: Sekhmet Disc Riders
parse_warnings: The current live list uses 'Sekhmet Disc Riders' while older source snippets use slightly different Thousand Sons reference wording.
ambiguity_reasons: The exact weapon naming is reconstructed from legacy compendium snippets.

profiles.name: Thousand Sons Neophyte
parse_warnings: Legacy compendium text is cramped and only partially exposes the Neophyte entry.
ambiguity_reasons: The source text is a compressed OCR snippet and the exact weapon naming is not fully explicit.

profiles.name: Thousand Sons Rhino
abilities_or_notes: May carry 2 of the following units: Thousand Sons Marines, Thousand Sons Adeptus, Thousand Sons Neophyte.

profiles.name: Thousand Sons Land Raider
abilities_or_notes: May carry 1 Thousand Sons Terminators or 2 of the following units: Thousand Sons Marines, Thousand Sons Adeptus, Thousand Sons Neophyte.

profiles.name: Thousand Sons Predator
parse_warnings: The current Thousand Sons compendium text compresses the Predator entry; the statline here follows the visible legacy snippet.
ambiguity_reasons: Weapon formatting is reconstructed from a legacy compendium OCR snippet.

profiles.name: Dreadclaw
abilities_or_notes: May transport one formation of infantry units and Chaos Dreadnoughts. The Dreadclaw does not scatter 2D6cm after being placed within 15cm of the drop zone co-ordinates recorded at the start of the game.

profiles.name: Daemon Prince of Tzeentch
abilities_or_notes: A Daemon Prince may have wings, the unit gains Jump Packs and a move of 30cm, while its armour value is reduced to 4+.

profiles.name: Tzeentch Horrors
parse_warnings: Tzeentch Horrors are reconstructed from the daemon pool text and older compendium references.
ambiguity_reasons: The local source files did not contain a Thousand Sons-specific Horror profile.

profiles.name: Tzeentch Screamers
parse_warnings: Tzeentch Screamers are reconstructed from the daemon pool text and older compendium references.
ambiguity_reasons: The local source files did not contain a Thousand Sons-specific Screamer profile.

profiles.name: Lord of Change
abilities_or_notes: Critical Hit Effect: The unit is destroyed, all summoned units within 5cm are destroyed on a roll of 6+.
parse_warnings: Boolean weapon line folded into previous weapon for Lord of Change.

profiles.name: Devastation Class Cruiser
abilities_or_notes: May transport 20 infantry units or Chaos Dreadnoughts; plus six Chaos Thunderhawks and enough Dreadclaws to transport any other units being carried.

profiles.name: Despoiler Class Battleship
abilities_or_notes: May transport 40 infantry units or Chaos Dreadnoughts; plus nine Chaos Thunderhawks and enough Dreadclaws to transport any other units being carried.

profiles.name: Greater Spires of Tzeentch
abilities_or_notes: Critical Hit Effect: Destroyed; each unit within 5cm takes a MW hit on a roll of 6.

profiles.name: Warp Palace of Tzeentch
abilities_or_notes: Critical Hit Effect: One of the Silver Runes that channel the power of the warp is shattered, releasing a storm of warp energy. It takes an extra point of damage; each unit within 5cm takes a MW hit on a roll of 6.

army_notes.name: List Notes
army_notes.text: This list is rated Under Development. List must contain at least one Rubric Fellowship formation.

army_notes.name: Formation Structure
army_notes.text: You may include any number of Retinues. In addition, formations may be given up to four upgrades and each upgrade may only be taken once per formation.

army_notes.name: Transport Warning
army_notes.text: If taking Dreadclaws, you must take just enough to transport the entire formation.

army_notes.name: Army Allegiance
army_notes.text: All formations in the army belong to the Chaos faction, Tzeentch.

army_notes.name: War Engines and Navy
army_notes.text: Up to a third of the army's points may be spent on Chaos Navy and War Engine formations.

extraction_warnings: Several Thousand Sons-specific profiles are reconstructed from the legacy NetEA compendium snippets because the local repository did not contain a Thousand Sons source JSON before this task.
Wraithgate is treated as a reference-only portal item and may render as a compact note card rather than a full unit profile card.

Sekhmet Disc Riders, Thousand Sons Neophyte, Thousand Sons Predator, Greater Spires of Tzeentch, and Warp Palace of Tzeentch are partially reconstructed from legacy compendium snippets and should be reviewed against a rendered PDF if one becomes available.
Tzeentch Horrors and Tzeentch Screamers are placeholder daemon pool profiles created so the builder can show a non-broken card for lesser daemon selections.
